title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · Memory Trace: Canary Rollback Observability
summary: A critical native agent must ship a memory rewrite with compatibility, rollback, observability, and practiced incident recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem canary rollback observability increment in production memory-safety defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Canary immutable artifacts against memory, crash, latency, allocator, and correctness signals with a tested rollback trigger.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Canary Rollback Observability · Theory Canary Rollback Observability
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 4817. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Build Canary Rollback Observability evidence · Theory Canary Rollback Observability
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-canary-rollback-observability, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-canary-rollback-observability-1ocoop */" for changed case 2196.
